name: Guadalén and La Fernandina Reservoirs Loop from La Carolina
name_original: Presa de La Fernandina y Embalse de Guadalén — circular desde La Carolina
description: Cycle a moderate 37.1-mile (59.7 km) loop from La Carolina, featuring the Guadalén Reservoir and Fernandina Dam with varied terrain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Carolina offer diverse terrain and elevation profiles, catering to various skill levels. The region is characterized by its varied landscapes, including forested areas and the presence of reservoirs. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse rolling hills and provide opportunities for significant elevation gain. This area provides a network of road cycling paths suitable for both moderate and challenging rides. Best road cycling routes around La Carolina The most popular road cycling route is La Carolina – return via Vilches and Embalse del Fernandina, a 24.5 miles (39.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete, featuring views of the Embalse del Fernandina reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Carolina, El Centenillo, and Burgalimar Castle Loop, a difficult 69.9 miles (112.4 km) path. This route includes substantial elevation gain and passes by Burgalimar Castle. Local road cyclists also love the Guadalén and La Fernandina Reservoirs Loop from La Carolina, a 37.1 miles (59.7 km) trail leading through areas with the Guadalén and La Fernandina Reservoirs, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around La Carolina is defined by forested hills, reservoirs, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in La Carolina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Carolina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around La Carolina?
A: The region of La Carolina, Spain, offers diverse terrain for road cyclists, characterized by rolling hills, forested areas, and significant elevation changes. You'll find routes that traverse varied landscapes, including those with views of reservoirs and historical landmarks, providing options for both moderate and challenging rides.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginner road cyclists in La Carolina?
A: Yes, La Carolina offers routes suitable for various skill levels, including beginners. While many routes feature significant elevation, there are options with less demanding profiles. For instance, out of the 20 available routes, 2 are classified as easy, providing a good starting point for those new to road cycling in the area.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling in La Carolina?
A: Road cycling routes in La Carolina often pass by significant natural features and historical landmarks. You can experience views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996022511/la-carolina-return-via-vilches-and-embalse-del-fernandina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carolina – return via Vilches and Embalse del Fernandina&lt;/a&gt; offer views of the Embalse del Fernandina reservoir, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996026071/la-carolina-el-centenillo-and-burgalimar-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carolina, El Centenillo, and Burgalimar Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by Burgalimar Castle.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available in La Carolina?
A: Yes, La Carolina features several circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1274431740/guadalen-and-la-fernandina-reservoirs-loop-from-la-carolina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalén and La Fernandina Reservoirs Loop from La Carolina&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 59.7 km and takes approximately 2 hours 48 minutes. Another challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de los Jardines – La Aliseda loop from La Carolina&lt;/a&gt;, spanning nearly 90 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in La Carolina, Spain?
A: Based on local cycling activity, the period between April and November is particularly popular for road cycling in La Carolina, Spain. During these months, the weather is generally favorable, making it ideal for exploring the region's diverse routes.

Q: How challenging are the road cycling routes in La Carolina?
A: The routes around La Carolina offer a range of difficulties. Out of approximately 20 routes, 10 are classified as moderate and 8 as difficult, featuring substantial elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996026071/la-carolina-el-centenillo-and-burgalimar-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carolina, El Centenillo, and Burgalimar Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with over 1700 meters of elevation gain over 112 km.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints in La Carolina?
A: Many routes in La Carolina provide scenic viewpoints. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic views from locations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;. These spots are often incorporated into longer rides, offering rewarding breaks with stunning vistas of the surrounding natural park.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in La Carolina?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in La Carolina, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes forested hills and scenic reservoirs, as well as the opportunities to pass by historical landmarks. The network of routes is appreciated for catering to different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that include historical sites or monuments?
A: Yes, several road cycling routes in La Carolina incorporate historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996026071/la-carolina-el-centenillo-and-burgalimar-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carolina, El Centenillo, and Burgalimar Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past the impressive Burgalimar Castle. Additionally, you might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5733357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vacas del Retamoso Rock Art&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's rich past.

Q: How long are the typical road cycling routes in La Carolina?
A: The road cycling routes in La Carolina vary significantly in length. You can find shorter rides, such as the 38 km 'El Empedraillo' route, or longer, more challenging options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996022511/la-carolina-return-via-vilches-and-embalse-del-fernandina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carolina – return via Vilches and Embalse del Fernandina&lt;/a&gt; is about 39.5 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadollano Roman Bridge – Monument Navas De Tolosa loop from La Carolina&lt;/a&gt; covers over 73 km.

Q: Are there any options for road cyclists looking for less crowded routes?
A: While specific 'less crowded' routes aren't explicitly highlighted, the region of La Carolina offers a network of routes that extend through natural parks and forested areas. Exploring some of the less popular, but still well-maintained, routes might provide a more secluded experience away from the main trails. The sheer number of available routes (over 20) suggests ample opportunity to find quieter paths.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 59.7 kilometers (37.1 miles) long and typically takes about 2 hours and 48 minutes to complete on a race bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Presa de La Fernandina y Embalse de Guadalén cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate. It involves an elevation gain of around 750 meters (2,460 feet), requiring good fitness. While much of it is well-paved, some segments may be unpaved and could present challenges for road cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly well-paved, making it suitable for race bikes. However, be aware that there might be some unpaved sections that could be more challenging. The route features varied landscapes, including both flatter sections and climbs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the Fernandina Dam and the Guadalén Reservoir and tower, which offer scenic views. You'll also pass through La Carolina, where you can see the Town Hall Square and Palace of the Intendant Olavide.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the start of the route in La Carolina?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting from La Carolina, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas near the Town Hall Square or other central locations in La Carolina to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Presa de La Fernandina y Embalse de Guadalén circular route. It's generally open for public access.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a race bike route of this distance and difficulty is not typically recommended. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured and consider the long distance and potential traffic.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Andalusia can get very hot in summer. For the most comfortable cycling experience, consider visiting during the spring (April-May) or autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the scenery is often at its best.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, Transandalus, Camino del Ferrocarril, and Plaza de la Aduana.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its 59.7-kilometer distance, 750 meters of elevation gain, and 'moderate' difficulty rating, this route is generally not recommended for absolute beginner road cyclists. It requires a good level of fitness and some experience with longer rides and climbs.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route starts and ends in La Carolina where amenities are available, specific cafes or dedicated water stops directly along the rural sections of the route are not guaranteed. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.
